summary: Review #2 covers the improvement cycle that addresses all three WARNINGs and the four actionable INFOs from berendt's external review #1. W-001 is fixed by extending TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations with all seven new CC-0084 validation paths (terminationGracePeriodSeconds, preStopSleepSeconds, harakiri, httpKeepAliveTimeout+httpKeepAlive, strategy.Recreate+RollingUpdate) and matching substring assertions. W-002 is fixed by deriving EXPECTED_REPLICAS from `kubectl get deploy ... -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}'` with an explicit empty-string guard. W-003 is fixed at both steady-state checkpoints (pre-rollout line 67 and post-rollout line 342) by replacing `(availableReplicas >= 3): true` with exact-match `availableReplicas: 3`. I-001 replaces the leftover reviewer prompt with a factual CI reference. I-002 rewrites the timing-budget comment to describe empirical per-cycle expectations rather than the incorrect arithmetic inequality. I-003 aligns the docs table rows for terminationGracePeriodSeconds/preStopSleepSeconds with the strategy/priorityClassName convention (Default: `nil`, reconciler-applied effective value documented in Effect). I-004 lifts the DECISION block out of the TerminationGracePeriodSeconds godoc into a preceding note separated by a blank line so controller-gen excludes it from the user-facing CRD description, and regenerates both CRD copies. Go tests pass (TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations + full package), go vet is clean, and the helm/config CRD pair is byte-identical for the shared block.

tests_checklist.item: Aggregate validation test updated with all new CC-0084 fields (W-001)
All new substring assertions present and passing (terminationGracePeriodSeconds, preStopSleepSeconds, harakiri, httpKeepAliveTimeout, strategy)
Tests compile (appsv1 import present) and pass under `go test ./api/v1alpha1/`
Full keystone test suite still passes
E2E availability script reads .spec.replicas dynamically with empty-value guard (W-002)
E2E steady-state asserts use exact-match equality at lines 67 and 342 (W-003)
Edge cases covered (empty jsonpath output triggers FAIL with clear message)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (matches CC-0075 aggregate-test structure and sibling chaos tests' kubectl jsonpath pattern)
No dead code or scope creep beyond addressing review #1 findings
Clear naming for test-local helpers (breakingHarakiri, breakingKeepAliveTimeout, grace, preStop)
CC-0084 feature ID referenced on every new comment, including back-references to review #1 finding IDs
godoc convention respected: internal-only Note block placed before the field's godoc and separated by blank line so controller-gen skips it

security_checklist.item: No new user input surfaces introduced
Webhook validation still rejects all invalid combinations (defense-in-depth preserved)
No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data introduced

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal and scoped to the five review findings
CRD helm/ and config/crd/bases/ remain byte-identical for the shared block (only header preamble differs)
Effective-default contract preserved: pointer remains optional, no +kubebuilder:default marker, reconciler applies 30/5 when nil
HA architecture doc deferred per I-006 (submodule rule); remaining acceptance criteria unchanged

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic; test setup reuses validKeystone() and pattern of existing CC-0075/CC-0065 aggregate assertions
Timing-budget comment rewrite is narrative-only; no runtime change

fail_fast_checklist.item: Empty `.spec.replicas` jsonpath output triggers immediate FAIL before polling loop begins
Aggregate test asserts HaveOccurred() up-front and every subsequent substring is evaluated (no short-circuit)

defensive_checklist.item: All runtime bounds checks on new CC-0084 fields remain in validate() (harakiri>=1, httpKeepAliveTimeout>=1, terminationGracePeriodSeconds>=10, preStopSleepSeconds>=0, cross-field rules)
DECISION block relocation did not alter any validation semantics

suggested_improvements: I-005 (type tests that verify Go struct/DeepCopy language semantics) remains open and is explicitly flagged ASK in review #1 — reaching team consensus on whether to delete TestKeystoneSpecTerminationGracePeriodSecondsField, TestKeystoneSpecStrategyField, TestUWSGISpecOptionalTimeoutFields (per CC-0013/CC-0078 precedent) or keep them as stale-deepcopy tripwires would close the remaining open thread from review #1.
I-006 HA architecture doc entry is deferred to a follow-up PR against the submodule; the PR description should mark it out-of-scope with a link to the follow-up ticket as recommended by berendt.
next_steps: Merge: all three WARNINGs and the four actionable INFOs (I-001..I-004) from review #1 are addressed; verdict is APPROVED.
File the HA architecture doc follow-up PR against the submodule so REQ-015 is not lost.
Decide on I-005 in the next triage (keep as tripwires vs delete per CC-0013/CC-0078 precedent).
